commits.subject: fix(mobile): eliminate bottom sheet lag by using GPU-composited transforms
commits.body:
The bottom sheet was animating CSS `height`, which triggers layout
recalculation (reflow) on every frame. During drag, every touchmove
called setState in App.jsx, re-rendering the entire component tree.

Fix: Sheet now uses a fixed height with `transform: translateY()` for
show/hide animation (GPU-composited, no layout reflow). During drag,
the DOM is updated directly via refs — React state only updates on
snap (touchend), eliminating mid-drag re-renders.

Tags: mobile, ui, performance, bottom-sheet
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: mobile-ux
Semver: patch

commits.subject: refactor(structure): unify cell selection with global selectedCell
commits.body:
Replaced the parallel structureSelection state system with the same
global selectedCell used by all other tabs. Section context (which
row/column a cell belongs to) is now derived via cellToSection() instead
of stored separately.

What changed:
- Removed internal CellGrid component (~200 lines), structureSelection
  state, lastInternalCell ref, and the sync useEffect
- Now uses MiniCellGrid (same as MediaTab, ContentTab, StyleTab)
- Section editing and cell subdivision editing are both shown based on
  the selected cell's derived section, no toggle needed
- All structural operations (swap, insert, remove, add/remove
  subdivision) simplified — just update global selectedCell directly

Why: The dual-state system caused sync bugs where ContextBar and other
tabs showed a different cell than the Structure tab. The sync logic was
complex (useEffect with ref guards, manual updates in 5 operations) and
still had edge cases. Using a single source of truth eliminates the
entire category of sync bugs.

swaps, inserts, and removals — but never updated the global selectedCell

Now all five structure-changing operations (swapSections, insertSection,
removeSection, addSubdivision, removeSubdivision) update the global

automatically adjust a cell's boundary so a contained image fills
the cell with no empty space and no cropping. The button appears
when a cell has an image set to "contain" fit mode.

The snap direction is determined by comparing the image's aspect
ratio to the cell's: wider images adjust the section boundary
(row height / column width), narrower images adjust the subdivision
boundary (cell width / cell height).

Image natural dimensions are now stored on upload for the calculation.
A fallback async load handles images uploaded before this feature.

viewer resampling that was degrading vignette quality.

commits.subject: fix(export): eliminate PDF viewer resampling that degraded vignette overlays
commits.body:
PDF vignette overlays looked ~30% quality vs 100% in PNG/JPG exports.
Root cause: pixelRatio:2 capture combined with 72/96 pt page conversion
forced PDF viewers to downsample the image. PDF viewers (especially
Chrome's) use cheap bilinear interpolation that destroys smooth gradients.

Fix: Use pixelRatio:1 (matching image exports) with 1:1 pixel-to-point
page dimensions. The embedded image maps directly to PDF page units with
no viewer resampling. Physical print dimensions are slightly off but
pixel quality now matches PNG/JPG exactly — correct tradeoff for digital.

Also simplified captureForPdf to always use PNG (lossless) and removed
unused JPEG quality/pixel budget constants from the investigation phase.

commits.subject: fix(export): fix PDF quality and file size by using JPEG instead of PNG
commits.body:
Root cause: jsPDF handles JPEG and PNG completely differently.
- JPEG: embedded directly as DCT_DECODE stream (no re-encoding)
- PNG: decoded to raw pixels (~23MB for 2160×2700), then re-compressed
  with deflate — producing files 5-10x larger than JPEG while being
  visually identical

Fix: Use JPEG at pixelRatio:2 with 0.92 quality. jsPDF wraps the JPEG
stream directly, giving sharp 2x rendering and small file sizes.
Transparency loss is irrelevant since PDF pages have white backgrounds.

Save/load/list/delete are now async with loading states and error feedback.

Backward compat cleanup: Text format migration (old global text → per-cell)
now only runs during the one-time localStorage→IndexedDB migration, not
on every design load. Designs stored in IndexedDB are already migrated.

Mobile touch targets: Increased minimum sizes across all interactive elements:
- ColorPicker: neutral swatches 14px → 24px, theme swatches 20px → 28px
- ContextBar: page buttons 20-24px → 28-32px, cell grid min-height 6px → 10px
- MiniCellGrid: cells 12-20px → 14-28px
- ContentTab: visibility/style toggles 24px → 28-32px, size/bold/italic buttons increased
- AlignmentPicker: buttons 20px → 24px
- All interactive elements: added active: states for touch feedback

iOS PWA: Added viewport-fit=cover for notched devices, changed status bar
style from black-translucent to default (prevents invisible text on light
backgrounds), added safe-area-inset padding.

Offline detection: New useOnlineStatus hook with amber banner when offline.
Tells users their work is saved locally but fonts/images may not load.

CSS cleanup: Removed duplicate overscroll-behavior-y from body and #root
(only needed on html). Removed redundant inline style from html tag.
Added min-height: 100dvh for dynamic viewport height on mobile.

useHistory optimization: Replaced full JSON.stringify dedup with smart
comparison that skips base64 image src data. Compares images by ID and
non-src fields only. Eliminates multi-MB serialization on every keystroke.

commits.subject: perf(export): use canvas.toBlob directly instead of fetch(dataUrl)
commits.body:
Previous approach for PNG/JPG: html-to-image → data URL → fetch() →
blob. The fetch(dataUrl) round-trips through the browser's network
stack to convert what is already in-memory base64 data.

New approach for all formats: html-to-image → canvas → canvas.toBlob().
This is what the WebP path already did. Now all three formats use the
same direct conversion, avoiding unnecessary Response object creation
and base64 encoding/decoding overhead.

Also drops the toJpeg import since toCanvas + canvas.toBlob('image/jpeg')
handles JPEG encoding natively.

Tags: export, performance

that calls clearInterval on unmount. The SW registration is stored

commits.subject: Fix reader mode canvas not using available space
commits.body:
The ResizeObserver tracking container width was set up with an empty
dependency array, so it only observed the editor mode's DOM element.
When switching to reader mode, the ref pointed to a new DOM element
but the observer was still watching the old (removed) one, causing
containerWidth to go stale. This made the canvas render much smaller
than the available viewport.

Adding isReaderMode to the dependency array ensures the observer
re-attaches to the correct DOM element on mode switch.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Mf3ye3eTc6NcnAQYzK82xE
commits.title: Fix reader mode canvas not using available space

commits.subject: Add semantic color tokens for light/dark mode
commits.body:
Created CSS variables and Tailwind tokens for consistent theming:

CSS Variables (in index.css):
- --color-text-default/muted/subtle/faint for text
- --color-surface/elevated/inset/hover for backgrounds
- --color-border/subtle/strong for borders

Tailwind Config (ui namespace):
- text-ui-text, text-ui-text-muted, etc.
- bg-ui-surface, bg-ui-surface-elevated, etc.
- border-ui-border, border-ui-border-subtle, etc.

Refactored components to use semantic classes instead of
paired light/dark classes (e.g., text-ui-text-muted instead
of text-zinc-600 dark:text-zinc-300).

This makes color maintenance easier and ensures consistency.

commits.subject: Fix export capturing scaled preview instead of full-size canvas
commits.body:
The canvas uses CSS transform scale for preview display, but html-to-image
was capturing the scaled-down version. Now temporarily sets scale(1) during
capture to export at correct dimensions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: Fix export capturing scaled preview instead of full-size canvas

commits.subject: Fix text layer state errors and Google Fonts export issue
commits.body:
Add defensive checks to prevent crashes when text layer state is
undefined (can happen during hot reload). Skip font embedding in
html-to-image export to avoid cross-origin stylesheet errors with
Google Fonts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: Fix text layer state errors and Google Fonts export issue

commits.subject: Add overlay to all layouts and expand text layers
commits.body:
- Fix overlay to apply to images in all layout types (background, vertical, horizontal)
- Expand text layers from 3 to 6: title, tagline, body heading, body text, call to action, footnote
- Update documentation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: Add overlay to all layouts and expand text layers

commits.subject: Add Social Ad Creator implementation
commits.body:
- Vite + React 18 + Tailwind CSS setup
- Image upload with drag-drop, object-fit and position controls
- 16 layout templates (background, vertical columns, horizontal rows)
- Text layers: title, subtitle, body with visibility and color controls
- 4 overlay types: solid, gradient down/up, vignette
- Theme system with 4 presets + custom colors
- 5 Google Fonts for title and body text
- Export to 6 platforms: LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter/X, TikTok
- Single download and batch ZIP download
- GitHub Pages deployment configured

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: Add Social Ad Creator implementation
